MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-14-2024 Vacated

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JONATHAN HARMS' Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs.

Granted as unopposed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Set For Trial On Aug-19-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2023 Continued To Jan-17-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Jordana Better, Esq. 310-351-4745 for Plaintiff Jonathan Harms who is also present.
Lawrence Hart, Esq. 510-457-3440 Law Offices of Scott C. Stratman for Defendant DOC RESTAURANT GROUP LLC DBA A12.

Argued. Te Court inquires re: late contest email. Ms. Better explains their office had computer issues. Mr. Hart objects. The Court allows Ms. Better to argue and instructs parties to file a regularly noticed motion re attorney's fees. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DOC RESTAURANT GROUP LLC DBA A12's Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment.

Defendants' Doc Restaurant Group LLC DBA A12 motion to set aside default judgment is granted.

Under 473(b), whenever an application for relief is made within six months after entry of judgment and is accompanied by an attorney's sworn affidavit attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect, the court must set aside the resulting default. (CCP 473(b).) Here, defense counsel Lawrence E. Hart has filed a sworn declaration attesting to his mistake and neglect in failing to answer plaintiff Jonathan Harms?s complaint. (See Declaration of Lawrence E. Hart, paragraphs 10-11.) The motion was filed within six months of the entry of the default. (Id. at paragraphs 5-7.) Accordingly, the court grants the motion to set aside the default.

Additionally, CCP 473(b) mandates that the court "shall, whenever relief is granted based on an attorney's affidavit of fault, direct the attorney to pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs to opposing counsel or parties." (CCP 473(b).) Plaintiff's counsel is to file a regularly noticed motion to determine the amount of legal fees and costs to be awarded.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
